subject: Computer Networking
title: Inside Email Headers
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Inside Email Headers.

hook.text: Every email has hidden metadata. Headers record the full journey, from origin to your inbox.

buildup.text: Headers include From, To, Subject, Date, and a unique Message-ID assigned by the sender's server.

discovery.text: Received headers are added at each hop. The bottom one is the origin, the top is the final server.

twist.text: Spammers forge From headers easily. Authentication headers like SPF and DKIM reveal the truth.

climax.text: Reply chains use In-Reply-To and References headers to thread messages. Your client reads these.

punchline.text: Headers are email's audit trail. Every hop leaves a stamp.

quiz.question: What do 'Received' headers in an email show?
options: Each server the email passed through on its journey
The number of times the email was opened
The attachments included in the email
